label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this. So, the question is asking which statement is the writer's opinion of the story. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to recall the story. The main character is a guy who has cancer and lives at home with his mom. He goes out one day and sees a girl in a music store. He's too shy to talk to her at first, but he buys a CD every day and the girl wraps it. He leaves his phone number once, and then he dies before she can respond. His mom finds out that each CD had a slip from the girl asking him out. The moral is about not waiting to express your feelings because life is uncertain. So the question is about the writer's message. The story ends with the line: "That's the way life is. Don't wait to show those special people the way you feel. Tomorrow would be too late." Looking at the options: A) Don't wait to show your feelings. This seems to directly match the ending message of the story. The last sentence says not to wait and that tomorrow might be too late. B) Life always goes like that. The story says "That's the way life is," but the main point isn't just that life is like this, but the action to take based on that. So B might be part of the setup for the moral, but the main opinion is more about taking action. C) There is only one chance for everyone. The story does mention the guy could have died at any moment, but the key message is more about acting on feelings rather than waiting, not necessarily about only one chance in general. The CD slips from Sophie indicate she was willing to give him chances, but he didn't act. D) Show your feelings in a proper way. The story doesn't focus on the method of showing feelings, but on the urgency and not waiting. The guy was too shy to ask her out, so the problem was not acting, not about how he
